title: Join UNICEF Nigeria’s Youth Action Team (YPAT) 2026: A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders
summary: The stage is set for a new generation of bold, passionate young leaders ready to make meaningful impact in their communities and beyond. If you are driven by purpose, committed to social change, and eager to grow your leadership capacity, the UNICEF GenU 9JA Young People’s Action Team (YPAT) 2026 is the opportunity you’ve been waiting for. This flagship initiative by UNICEF Nigeria is designed to empower young people to actively participate in shaping policies, driving community-based projects, and influencing decisions that affect their future. Why You Should Apply The YPAT program is not just another volunteer opportunity—it is a transformative leadership journey. Selected participants will collaborate directly with UNICEF teams, development partners, and policymakers to address critical issues such as education, climate action, digital innovation, health, and WASH. Participants will gain hands-on experience and contribute to real-world solutions that impact communities at local, national, and global levels. What You’ll Gain Joining YPAT opens the door to a wide range of personal and professional development opportunities: Practical experience in youth advocacy and governance Skills in project planning and community engagement Training in digital content creation and social campaigns Leadership development, mentoring, and peer mobilization Exposure to policy dialogue and youth participation strategies Access to a nationwide network of changemakers and mentors Key Responsibilities As a member of the Young People’s Action Team, you will: Represent youth voices in governance and policy discussions Lead and support community-based initiatives Organize and participate in youth-focused events and dialogues Create impactful digital content for social change Mentor peers and mobilize youth networks Participate in UNICEF-led campaigns and training programs Eligibility Criteria To be considered for this opportunity, applicants must: Be a Nigerian citizen Be between 10 and 29 years old Be passionate about youth empowerment and social impact Be willing to commit to a 12-month program (both online and in-person) Have access to a smartphone and internet connection Be proficient in English How to Apply Interested candidates can submit their application through the official link below. The application process takes approximately 20–30 minutes to complete. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more volunteering opportunities, click here . Application Deadline All applications must be submitted on or before 25 April 2026 . Early submission is strongly encouraged, as no deadline extension will be granted. Final Thoughts The UNICEF YPAT program is more than a volunteer role—it is a platform to amplify your voice, build your skills, and contribute to meaningful change. If you are ready to step into leadership, influence policy, and empower your peers, this is your moment. Do not sit on the sidelines. Step into the room and take action toward shaping a better future. title: Write Your Way to Global Impact: Apply for the 2026 Emerging Perspectives Short Essay Competition (Win $1,000 + Publication)
summary: The 2026 Emerging Perspectives Short Essay Competition is officially open, offering young writers a powerful platform to share their voices on one of the most pressing global issues of our time—migration in the age of polycrisis. Organized by the Mixed Migration Centre, this annual competition invites fresh, original perspectives from emerging thinkers across the Global South. If you have a story, insight, or lived experience about how overlapping global crises are shaping migration today, this is your opportunity to be heard globally—and rewarded for it. Why This Competition Matters We are living in an era defined by interconnected crises—climate change, conflict, economic instability, and geopolitical shifts. Together, these forces are transforming migration patterns worldwide. The Mixed Migration Review 2026 (MMR 2026) will explore how this “polycrisis” is reshaping human mobility. This competition is your chance to contribute directly to that global conversation. What You’ll Gain Participating in this competition offers more than just recognition: Global Publication : Winning essays will be featured in the prestigious Mixed Migration Review 2026 Cash Prize : Each of the five winners will receive USD $1,000 International Exposure : Share your voice with policymakers, researchers, and global audiences Career Boost : Strengthen your portfolio as a writer, researcher, or storyteller Competition Theme: Migration in the Age of Polycrisis Applicants are encouraged to explore how overlapping global crises are influencing migration. Possible angles include: How intersecting crises affect migration decisions and experiences The politicization of migration in your region Climate change as a driver of migration Shifting migration policies and border control measures Labour migration realities Changes in humanitarian protection systems Your submission should focus on original storytelling, personal insights, or underrepresented perspectives rather than academic theory. Submission Format The application process happens in two stages: Stage 1: Abstract Submission Submit a maximum 200-word abstract outlining your essay idea Stage 2: Full Essay (if selected) Selected applicants will be invited to submit a 1,500-word essay Important Deadline Abstract submission deadline: May 5, 2026 Invitations for full essays: May 22, 2026 Final essay submission: June 22, 2026 Winners announced: July 13, 2026 How to Apply Submit your abstract through the official application form. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more competition opportunities, click here . Key Rules to Keep in Mind Only one abstract per applicant is allowed Co-authored essays are permitted (prize awarded per essay) AI-generated content is strictly prohibited and will lead to disqualification Essays must not exceed 1,500 words What Makes a Winning Entry? The selection team is looking for: Authentic, personal, or locally grounded perspectives Creative storytelling and compelling narratives Fresh insights on underreported migration issues Practical or innovative ideas for improving migration policies Final Thoughts This is more than a writing competition—it’s a chance to shape global understanding of migration through your lived experience and ideas. If you’ve ever wanted your voice to contribute to meaningful change, now is the time. Submit your abstract, tell your story, and become part of a global dialogue that matters. title: Afrobarometer Assistant Project Manager Job in Ghana (2026) – Apply Before April 17
summary: If you re an experienced research professional with a passion for data-driven development across Africa, the Afrobarometer Assistant Project Manager role offers a unique opportunity to lead impactful survey projects across multiple countries. Based in Accra, Ghana, this full-time position places you at the heart of one of Africa’s most respected research networks. About the Role The Assistant Project Manager (APM) will oversee Afrobarometer’s regional survey operations in selected African countries. Working closely with senior leadership, including the Core Partner Project Manager and Survey Directorate, the APM ensures that all survey activities meet strict Afrobarometer protocols and timelines. This role is ideal for professionals who thrive in project management, research coordination, and international collaboration. Key Responsibilities 1. Survey Management Coordinate with National Partners (NPs) to develop survey schedules Review budgets and ensure cost-effectiveness Manage sub-contracts and authorize payments Review deliverables such as questionnaires, translations, and reports Ensure compliance with Afrobarometer survey protocols Provide technical support and training to partners 2. Dissemination Communication Oversee dissemination of survey findings Promote research through workshops, conferences, and publications Support press releases and global result launches 3. Network Partnership Development Evaluate and recruit new National Partners Support special research projects Participate in committees and strategic initiatives 4. Leadership Strategy Build stakeholder relationships across Africa Contribute to Afrobarometer’s strategic growth Encourage innovation and performance excellence 5. Monitoring Reporting Update monitoring and evaluation systems Prepare technical and financial reports Participate in advisory and survey unit meetings 6. Fundraising Support local fundraising efforts for survey activities Location Accra, Ghana Employment Type Full-time Required Qualifications Postgraduate degree (preferably in social sciences) Minimum of 3 years’ experience in survey research (NGO, public sector, or academia) Strong analytical, research, and statistical skills (SPSS required) Excellent communication and presentation skills Proven project management experience Ability to work in multicultural environments Willingness to travel across Africa Why Apply? Work on high-impact research influencing policy across Africa Gain international exposure and leadership experience Collaborate with top researchers and institutions Contribute to evidence-based governance and development How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their CV and cover letter via email: 📧 jobs@cddgh.org Application Deadline April 17, 2026 APPLY HERE. title: Master Collective Bargaining Skills Anytime, Anywhere with the “Bargaining Voices” Free WhatsApp Course
summary: In today’s fast-paced professional environment, mastering negotiation skills—especially in collective bargaining—is more important than ever. Whether you represent workers, employers, or manage industrial relations, having the ability to navigate complex negotiations can significantly impact outcomes and relationships. Introducing Bargaining Voices , an innovative, mobile-first learning experience designed to strengthen your negotiation capabilities—delivered directly through WhatsApp. What is Bargaining Voices? Bargaining Voices is a free, interactive course developed by the International Training Centre of the ILO (ITCILO), through a collaboration between the Workers’ Activities Programme (ACTRAV) and the Employers’ Activities Programme (ACTEMP). This course transforms learning into a dynamic experience through 10 short, engaging “learning pills” that simulate real-life negotiation scenarios. Participants are guided through storytelling, podcast-style content, and practical exercises that allow them to step into the shoes of negotiators and refine their decision-making skills. Why You Should Enroll 1. 100% Free Access Gain access to high-quality, expert-designed training at no cost. 2. Learn on the Go The course is delivered via WhatsApp, allowing you to learn anytime, anywhere—perfect for busy professionals. 3. Short and Impactful Complete the entire course in just 4–5 hours , broken into manageable modules. 4. Practical, Real-World Learning Engage with realistic negotiation scenarios that help you apply concepts immediately in your professional context. 5. Earn a Digital Badge Upon completion, receive a digital badge to showcase your newly acquired skills on your professional profiles. What You’ll Learn Bargaining Voices is designed to strengthen essential skills required for effective collective bargaining, including: Preparation strategies for negotiations Crafting strong opening statements Managing pressure during discussions Problem-solving in complex situations Reaching sustainable and mutually beneficial agreements Who Should Take This Course? This course is ideal for: Trade union representatives Employers’ organization staff HR professionals Industrial relations practitioners Anyone involved in or preparing for collective negotiations A Unique Learning Experience Unlike traditional courses, Bargaining Voices leverages WhatsApp as its primary platform. Each learning module is self-paced and designed to fit seamlessly into your daily routine. This approach ensures accessibility and flexibility without compromising on quality. As Vera Dos Santos Costa, Manager of the Workers’ Activities Programme, highlights, the course brings “the reality of negotiations into learners’ hands,” offering practical tools that can be immediately applied. Deadline There is no specified deadline , and participants are encouraged to enroll and start learning at their convenience. How to Get Started Take the next step in strengthening your negotiation skills and enhancing your professional impact. Enroll in Bargaining Voices today and start building your voice at the negotiation table. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more short courses opportunities, click here . title: Fully Funded PhD Scholarships at Western Sydney University (Australia) – Apply for Q3 2026 Intake
summary: Western Sydney University has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Research Scholarships for the Quarter 3 (Q3) 2026 intake. This is an excellent opportunity for both domestic and international students looking to pursue a fully funded doctoral degree in Australia. With generous financial support, access to world-class research facilities, and the chance to work alongside leading academics, this scholarship program is designed to support high-achieving candidates committed to advancing knowledge and innovation. 🎓 About the Scholarship Program Western Sydney University offers a range of research scholarships aimed at supporting Higher Degree Research (HDR) candidates. These scholarships are awarded on a competitive basis and are released periodically throughout the year. For the Q3 2026 round, applicants must first submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) before being invited to complete a full application. 📅 Important Dates Applications Open: Wednesday, 01 April 2026 Applications Close: Thursday, 30 April 2026 (5:00 PM AEST) Program Commencement: Monday, 22 June 2026 Late or incomplete applications will not be considered, so it is crucial to submit all required documents before the deadline. 📚 Eligible Programs Scholarships are available for the following doctoral-level programs: Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) Professional Doctorates (DCA, DCR, EdD) 💰 Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive: Annual stipend: AUD $35,188 for up to three years Full tuition coverage under the Australian Government Research Training Program (RTP) ❌ Not Covered: Travel expenses Visa fees Relocation costs Other personal expenses ✅ Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: General Eligibility: Be a new applicant applying for a doctoral program; OR Have commenced a doctoral program in 2026; OR Hold an offer to begin in Q3 2026 Additional Requirements for International Applicants: Must have completed a Master of Philosophy (MPhil) or Master of Research (MRes) at Western Sydney University in 2025 or 2026 All Applicants Must: Be able to study full-time Be physically present in Australia at the start of the program Provide a letter of support from a supervisor 📝 Application Process For New Applicants: Apply online via the university application portal Select the Q3 2026 Scholarship Release option Submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) Await invitation to submit a full application For Current PhD Candidates (2026 intake): Notify your Director of HDR Submit any additional documents as requested 📌 Key Notes Applications are highly competitive Incomplete or late submissions will not be reviewed Scholarships are awarded by a university-appointed ranking panel Applicants must dedicate at least 35 hours per week to their research Limited work is allowed, provided it does not interfere with study 🔍 Why Choose Western Sydney University? Globally recognized research institution Strong industry and academic collaborations Diverse and inclusive research environment Access to cutting-edge facilities and expert supervision APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more PhD Scholarships opportunities, click here . 🚀 Final Thoughts If you are passionate about research and ready to take the next step in your academic journey, this fully funded PhD scholarship at Western Sydney University is a golden opportunity. Make sure to prepare your documents early, secure a supervisor, and submit your application before the deadline. title: Program Coordinator at Emory University (USA): Launch Your Career in International Education
summary: Are you an early-career professional passionate about global engagement, international education, and building cross-border partnerships? The Program Coordinator position at Emory Global Engagement (EGE) offers an exceptional opportunity to grow your career while contributing to impactful global initiatives at one of the world’s leading research universities. About the Opportunity Emory University is renowned for fostering innovation, academic excellence, and global collaboration. Through Emory Global Engagement, the institution strengthens international partnerships, supports global research, and welcomes students and scholars from around the world. As a Program Coordinator, you will play a central role in advancing these initiatives while gaining hands-on experience in international program management and partnership development. Key Responsibilities In this role, you will: Support global partnership projects, including tracking systems and data management Assist in managing partnership agreements and Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs) Coordinate high-level meetings, international visits, and leadership travel logistics Plan and execute major events such as International Education Week and the International Awards celebration Contribute to fundraising strategies and identify funding opportunities Prepare operational and statistical reports Provide general administrative and programmatic support Minimum Qualifications To be considered, applicants must have: A Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field At least two years of related experience (or equivalent combination of education and experience) Preferred Qualifications Strong candidates will demonstrate: Experience in event planning and coordination Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously Familiarity with tools like Microsoft Excel and project management platforms (e.g., Asana) Interest or experience in international and intercultural work Work Environment This is primarily an in-person role based at Emory University. Limited remote work flexibility may be offered at the department’s discretion. Why You Should Apply Working at Emory University provides: A dynamic, globally focused work environment Opportunities to collaborate with international partners and leaders Career growth in the field of international education Exposure to high-impact global programs and initiatives Equal Opportunity Statement Emory University is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without discrimination based on race, gender, religion, disability, or other protected characteristics. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through Emory University’s official careers portal by creating an online profile, uploading their resume, and completing the application process. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . Application Deadline No specific deadline has been stated. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged. title: ASEFYLS 2026 Capacity Training: Fully Funded Youth Leadership Program (Apply Now)
summary: Are you a young leader eager to shape the future and create meaningful change in your community? The ASEF Young Leaders Summit (ASEFYLS) Capacity Training 2026 is now accepting applications for its latest cohort. This dynamic programme empowers youth from Asia and Europe to develop leadership skills, explore future-thinking strategies, and drive real-world impact. What is ASEFYLS? The ASEF Young Leaders Summit (ASEFYLS) is a youth platform designed to build a strong network of young leaders across Asia and Europe. It offers a transformative learning journey focused on: Self-leadership (You) Team leadership (We) Societal leadership (All) The programme fosters curiosity, innovation, and intercultural understanding—equipping participants with the tools to become forward-thinking leaders who can drive sustainable and inclusive change. At the heart of ASEFYLS 2026 is the concept of Anticipatory Leadership —the ability to use insights about the future to inform present decisions. This includes analyzing trends, identifying emerging signals, and adapting leadership approaches to build more equitable and sustainable societies. Programme Theme: Anticipatory Leadership for a Human-Centred Future The 2026 edition will focus on “Anticipatory Leadership for a Human-Centred Future.” Participants will explore how to: Analyze trends and weak signals Apply strategic foresight and systems thinking Understand the ethical implications of emerging technologies like AI Lead with inclusivity and sustainability in mind Why You Should Apply Learn Gain insights from leading institutions, thematic experts, and high-level speakers on future-focused leadership. Network Connect with like-minded young leaders from across Asia and Europe and build long-term partnerships. Take Action Apply your knowledge in your local community through impactful grassroots initiatives. What to Expect in ASEFYLS 2026 The programme runs from June to November 2026 and includes the following phases: 1. Training Phase (Online) 9 June – 30 July 2026 Participate in interactive online sessions (approx. 4 hours per week) Learn about anticipatory leadership, systems thinking, and foresight tools Explore how technology influences ethical and inclusive leadership 2. Action Phase (Local Implementation) August – November 2026 Organize multiplier events in your local community Share knowledge and drive impact at the grassroots level Translate theory into real-world action 3. Optional In-Person Training (Selected Participants) Asia-Europe Youth Leadership Training: Anticipatory Leadership in the Arctic Tromsø, Norway 23 – 29 August 2026 40 participants will be selected based on engagement Dive deeper into anticipatory leadership using the Arctic as a case study Engage in cross-cultural exchange and expert dialogues Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be citizens of eligible Asian or European countries Be 18–30 years old (students or young professionals) Be actively involved in youth or student organizations Demonstrate leadership and community engagement Have strong English communication skills Selection will be based on motivation, leadership experience, and potential to create impact in local communities. Programme Benefits No registration or participation fees Travel subsidies (for selected participants) Accommodation and meals covered during in-person training Access to a global network of young leaders Hands-on leadership and future-thinking training Commitment Required Participants are expected to: Attend 2 online sessions per week during the training phase Complete self-paced assignments Organize community-based multiplier events (If selected) Participate in the in-person training in Norway How to Apply Interested candidates can submit their applications through the official programme portal. APPLY HERE! Deadline 12 May 2026! Pro Tip Highlight your community impact and leadership experience in your application. Showcase how you plan to apply anticipatory leadership in solving real challenges in your local context. Register Here, For FAQ Webinar ASEFYLS 2026 is more than just a training programme—it’s a platform to reimagine the future and actively shape it . If you re passionate about leadership, innovation, and making a difference, this is your opportunity to join a global movement of changemakers. Discover similar opportunities here. title: Democracy Camp 2026 Germany: A Transformative Youth Program on Democracy, Sustainability, and European Participation
summary: The Democracy Camp 2026 in Germany is a five-day international youth exchange program designed to empower young people to actively engage with democracy, sustainability, intersectionality, and European cooperation. Taking place in Vallendar, Germany, this immersive experience brings together 60 young participants from across Europe to explore how democratic systems function at different levels and how youth can meaningfully influence political and social change. This program is not just a learning experience it is a collaborative space where participants co-create ideas, develop civic engagement skills, and contribute to a tangible output: the Youth Participation Handbook, a policy-oriented publication aimed at improving youth inclusion in democratic processes across Europe. Overview of Democracy Camp 2026 Democracy Camp 2026 is structured as an intercultural training and dialogue platform that connects young people aged 16 to 26 from multiple European countries. The camp focuses on bridging the gap between youth and decision-making institutions while strengthening understanding of democracy in practice. Participants will engage in interactive workshops, group discussions, field visits, and collaborative projects. The program is built around real-world challenges such as climate change, social inequality, inclusion, and democratic participation at local, national, and European levels. The event will take place from 17 June to 21 June 2026 in Haus der Familie, Vallendar, Germany, with a digital preparatory session held in May 2026. Core Themes and Learning Objectives The Democracy Camp 2026 is built around two central guiding questions: What participation tools are available for young people today? How can these tools be used to promote sustainability, inclusion, and democratic engagement? Through these questions, participants will explore key thematic areas: Democracy and Political Participation Participants will gain a deeper understanding of democratic systems, from local governance to European Union structures and global institutions such as the United Nations. The camp emphasizes practical participation tools, including advocacy, civic campaigns, and youth-led initiatives. Sustainability and Climate Action The program highlights the relationship between democracy and sustainability, encouraging participants to examine how political decisions affect environmental outcomes and how youth can influence climate policy. Intersectionality and Inclusion A major focus is placed on social justice, equality, and inclusion. Participants will explore how intersecting identities influence access to political participation and how inclusive democracies can be strengthened. Youth Empowerment and Leadership The camp develops leadership, communication, and project management skills, enabling participants to design and implement impactful civic initiatives in their home countries. Program Structure and Activities The Democracy Camp 2026 is carefully structured into three phases: 1. Digital Kick-Off Session (26 May 2026) Participants will meet online before the camp begins to share ideas, set expectations, and connect with facilitators and fellow participants. This session introduces key themes and allows participants to shape the agenda. 2. In-Person Camp in Vallendar (17–21 June 2026) The main camp includes intensive workshops, intercultural exchange activities, and collaborative sessions. Participants will explore democratic practices across Europe, analyze case studies, and develop practical participation tools. A key highlight includes a study visit to Bonn, where participants will engage with the United Nations Campus and meet local civic initiatives working on democratic participation and sustainability. Evenings will be dedicated to reflection, networking, and intercultural exchange activities, culminating in a final celebration event. 3. Post-Camp Publication and Impact Phase After the camp, participants will co-author the Youth Participation Handbook 2026. This publication will include recommendations, tools, and insights designed to improve youth participation across Europe. The handbook will be shared with policymakers, including members of the European Parliament, to influence future youth engagement strategies. Costs and Financial Support One of the most attractive aspects of Democracy Camp 2026 is its full financial coverage: Participation is completely free of charge Travel reimbursement up to 250 EUR is provided Accommodation and meals are fully covered Vegetarian and vegan meals are provided throughout the program Participants are encouraged to use environmentally friendly travel options such as trains, buses, or carpooling, which are prioritized in the selection process. Accommodation and Facilities The camp will be hosted at Haus der Familie in Vallendar, Germany. The venue offers a comfortable and interactive environment with facilities such as sports fields, recreational spaces, and group activity areas. These facilities are designed to support both structured learning and informal intercultural exchange. Who Can Apply Applicants must meet the following requirements: Age between 16 and 26 years Ability to communicate in English for the duration of the program Willingness to engage in intercultural dialogue and teamwork Interest in democracy, sustainability, and civic participation Applications from young people with fewer opportunities are strongly encouraged and prioritized to ensure diversity and inclusion. Minors are welcome but must provide parental consent and be accompanied according to program safety guidelines. Application Deadline The application deadline for Democracy Camp 2026 is 16 March 2026. Why You Should Apply Democracy Camp 2026 offers a rare opportunity to: Connect with young leaders from across Europe Develop real-world civic engagement skills Understand European and global democratic systems Contribute to a policy-relevant publication Build a network of changemakers Gain hands-on experience in international collaboration This program is ideal for young people who are passionate about democracy, climate action, social justice, and international cooperation. Final Thoughts Democracy Camp 2026 is more than a youth exchange it is a platform for shaping the future of democratic participation in Europe. By bringing together diverse voices and ideas, the program empowers young people to become active contributors to policy discussions and social transformation. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities click here The post Democracy Camp 2026 Germany: A Transformative Youth Program on Democracy, Sustainability, and European Participation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Careers at the Center for Global Development (CGD): Jobs, Internships & Fellowships to Tackle Global Poverty
summary: If you are passionate about solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges— poverty, inequality, and sustainable development —then a career at the Center for Global Development (CGD) could be the opportunity you’ve been looking for. CGD is a globally recognized think tank dedicated to producing high-impact research and policy solutions that improve lives in developing countries. Through its careers, internships, and fellowships programs, CGD offers professionals and early-career talents the chance to work at the intersection of research, policy, and global impact . Why Work at the Center for Global Development? At CGD, people are at the heart of everything. The organization places strong emphasis on: Intellectual excellence and innovation Diversity, equity, and inclusion Collaboration and professional growth Real-world policy impact Employees are not just part of a workplace—they are part of a mission-driven community working to reduce global poverty and inequality through evidence-based solutions. Current Job and Internship Opportunities CGD regularly recruits talented individuals across different levels and areas of expertise. Current openings include: 1. Institutional Advancement Internship – Washington, DC An excellent entry point for students or recent graduates interested in: Development fundraising and partnerships Nonprofit operations Communications and donor engagement APPLY HERE 2. Research Assistant – Gender Equality (Temporary) – Washington, DC A role suited for candidates with strong analytical skills and interest in: Gender equality and social policy Development research Data analysis and policy evaluation APPLY HERE 📌 Important Note: Due to the high volume of applications, CGD only contacts shortlisted candidates for interviews. Life at CGD: A Dynamic and Inclusive Work Environment CGD is known for fostering a workplace culture that is: Collaborative and collegial Intellectually stimulating Supportive of innovation and growth Employees benefit from a vibrant environment that combines serious policy work with opportunities for social interaction and well-being. Benefits for U.S.-Based Employees Full-time employees in the United States enjoy a highly competitive benefits package, including: Comprehensive health and dental insurance 6 weeks of paid time off (PTO) annually 15 paid holidays Employer-paid life and disability insurance Generous parental leave Retirement contribution (15%) after one year Onsite fitness center and wellness programs Tuition reimbursement opportunities Social activities such as sports leagues, events, and team gatherings Benefits for CGD Europe (UK-Based Staff) For employees based in CGD Europe, benefits include: 25 days annual leave + bank holidays Enhanced pension contributions Family and parental support benefits Health and insurance coverage Learning and development funding Flexible work arrangements (minimum 3 days in office) Cycle-to-work scheme Staff support networks and wellbeing initiatives Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion CGD is deeply committed to building an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued and respected. All applicants are considered without discrimination based on : Race or ethnicity Gender identity or expression Sexual orientation Religion or disability Age or veteran status This commitment ensures that CGD continues to benefit from diverse perspectives and experiences , which are essential to solving global challenges. Career Growth and Learning Opportunities Working at CGD is not just about a job—it’s about continuous growth. Employees have access to: Professional development programs Research collaborations with global experts Opportunities to contribute to influential policy debates Mentorship and career advancement pathways Why Choose CGD? Joining CGD means becoming part of a global effort to: Reduce poverty Promote equality Influence international development policy Create long-term, sustainable change Whether you are starting your career or looking to advance in the development sector, CGD offers a platform to make meaningful impact at a global scale . title: New American Speakers Program 2026 (Cohort 10): Fully Funded Storytelling & Public Speaking Training for Refugees in the U.S.
summary: The Refugee Storytellers Collective is now accepting applications for the New American Speakers Program (NASP) – Cohort 10 , a highly impactful initiative designed to empower refugees and immigrants in the United States to reclaim their narratives, amplify their voices, and become influential storytellers and advocates . In a world where migration narratives are often shaped by misinformation or oversimplification, this program offers something powerful: the opportunity for those with lived experience to tell their own stories—on their own terms . Why This Program Matters More Than Ever Refugees and immigrants are frequently spoken about—but rarely given the platform to speak for themselves. Misconceptions, stereotypes, and political narratives often dominate public discourse. The New American Speakers Program directly challenges this by: Centering authentic lived experiences Training participants to become confident public speakers Equipping them to influence public opinion and policy conversations Building a network of storytellers who advocate for more inclusive and accurate narratives This is not just a training—it is a movement toward narrative justice and representation . A Deep Dive into the Program Experience The NASP Cohort 10 is a 10-week immersive journey (May 11 – July 24, 2026) , delivered entirely online to ensure accessibility for participants across the United States. What You Will Learn Participants go far beyond basic public speaking. The program is carefully designed to develop a wide range of skills: 🎤 1. Public Speaking Mastery Overcoming fear and building confidence Structuring and delivering impactful speeches Engaging diverse audiences ✍️ 2. Storytelling Narrative Building Transforming personal experiences into compelling narratives Identifying key themes and messages Balancing emotion, clarity, and purpose 🌍 3. Advocacy Leadership Using storytelling as a tool for social change Speaking in policy, academic, and community spaces Representing refugee and immigrant voices responsibly 🤝 4. Collaboration Community Building Learning alongside peers with shared experiences Giving and receiving feedback in a safe environment Building long-term connections and support systems A Safe and Empowering Learning Environment One of the most unique aspects of this program is its intentional design as a safe and inclusive space . Participants are: Surrounded by individuals with shared lived experiences of displacement Encouraged to express themselves without fear of judgment Supported by facilitators who understand the emotional and social complexities of storytelling This environment is essential for enabling participants to open up, reflect deeply, and grow confidently . Storytelling Nights: Where Learning Meets Real Impact A standout feature of the program is the Storytelling Nights —live events where participants present their stories to public audiences. 📅 Tentative Dates: June 17, 2026 July 23, 2026 These sessions are more than practice—they are: Real opportunities to engage with audiences Platforms to challenge stereotypes and inspire change Moments where participants step into their role as public advocates and speakers What Happens After the Program? Graduating from NASP is not the end—it is just the beginning. Successful participants will: Receive a certificate of completion Become official members of the Refugee Storytellers Collective Gain access to an exclusive network of speaking opportunities Be connected with event organizers, institutions, and platforms seeking speakers This means your story doesn’t just stay in the classroom—it reaches the world. Financial Accessibility: A Fully Funded Opportunity All selected participants may receive a scholarship covering the full cost of the program , making this opportunity accessible regardless of financial background. This ensures that talent, voice, and potential—not financial means—determine participation . Who Should Apply? This program is ideal for individuals who: Have experienced forced displacement (refugees, asylum seekers, etc.) Are currently based in the United States Want to improve their public speaking and storytelling skills Are passionate about advocacy, representation, and social impact Priority Consideration Special attention will be given to applicants who are: Veterans Business owners Faith leaders These individuals often hold influential roles in their communities and can amplify impact even further. Application Process: How to Stand Out The application process is designed to understand your story, motivation, and potential . Steps: Complete the online application form Provide detailed responses about your experiences and goals Participate in a Zoom interview if shortlisted Tips for a Strong Application: Be authentic and honest —your lived experience is your strength Clearly explain why storytelling matters to you Show your willingness to engage, learn, and grow Highlight any past experiences in communication, leadership, or advocacy (if applicable) Important Dates to Remember 📅 Application Deadline: April 17, 2026 🚀 Program Start Date: May 11, 2026 🎤 Program End Date: July 24, 2026 The Bigger Impact: Changing Narratives, One Story at a Time The New American Speakers Program is not just about individual growth—it contributes to a broader mission: Shifting public narratives around migration Promoting empathy and understanding Building bridges between communities Empowering underrepresented voices Every story shared has the potential to educate, inspire, and transform perspectives . Final Thoughts If you have ever felt that your story deserves to be heard—but didn’t know how to share it—this program is your opportunity. The New American Speakers Program offers more than skills. It offers: A platform A community A voice And most importantly, it offers the chance to turn your personal journey into a force for change .
